name: Battlefield™ V
description: This is the ultimate Battlefield V experience. Enter mankind’s greatest conflict with the complete arsenal of weapons, vehicles, and gadgets plus the best customization content of Year 1 and 2.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS: 64-bit Windows 10
  • Processor: AMD FX-8350/ Core i5 6600K
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GeForce® GTX 1050 / NVIDIA GeForce® GTX 660 2GB or AMD Radeon™ RX 560 / HD 7850 2GB
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Storage: 50 GB available space
comment1:
Don't normalize external launchers, stop cluttering my disk with useless crap and horrible broken external launchers.
Just launch my game.
comment2: "Battlefield V" despite all the criticism it received over the years is still for me one of the best multiplayer war games ever created. Once again I am focusing mostly on the multiplayer because that is where Battlefield always delivered its best moments. Battlefield V captured that feeling of large scale war incredibly well, the explosions, constant chaos, planes flying overhead, tanks pushing through destroyed buildings and bullets flying everywhere around your teammates made every match feel intense and cinematic. The graphics and sound design were absolutely beautiful and together created moments that honestly felt like scenes straight out of a war movie. What I miss the most in modern shooters is exactly this kind of atmosphere, because back then games still tried to immerse you in war instead of constantly selling flashy colorful skins that completely destroy immersion. Battlefield V despite its flaws still kept a lot of that raw battlefield feeling where the focus was on the atmosphere, teamwork and the chaos of war itself. For me Battlefield 1 and Battlefield V are still two of the greatest multiplayer war games ever made because they understood how important immersion and atmosphere really are.
comment3:
this game is not as good as battlefield 1, the gameplay is a lot more modern fps shooter-like with fast TTK and op run and gun SMGs. there are less players than battlefield 1, however still plenty enough to find games. vehicles spawn a lot and they are very OP. 
some of the worst aspects of this game is the fact that there is a lack of factions, there is only Germany, USA, Japan, and UK, almost every ww2 game should have the russian faction since they have so many iconic weapons and vehicles, however this is because support was cut short for this game i think. 
theres also random weird skins and women soldiers that ruin the immersion, if there was a soviet faction then women soldiers would make sense, but having german women and japanese women as soldiers is crazy since they were ultra conservative countries that didn't want women fighting in war.
finally, there arent many weapons and equipment in the game.
overall, its an average ww2 shooter, i still play it sometimes since its the only battlefield ww2 fps game, its the third playable battlefield behind bf1 and bf6.
